title: Baker's Lisbon Coronation
theme: Jon Baker's wire-to-wire double: from the second hole of round two he led the Trophy and never gave it back, gathering the Green Jacket along the way while the rest of the field auditioned for the Wooden Spoon.
opening_hook: Open on Royal Óbidos, R3 holes 11–16, as Jon Baker piles 20 points onto an already healthy lead (b04) — the moment the tournament stopped being a contest. Then flash back to how we got there.
foreshadow: Patterson's brief R1 lead, plant it early as the last time anyone other than Jon Baker would top the Trophy board
Tease Gregg Williams' opening-round Gross lead (b under jacket arc) as a mirage that curdles into a Spoon
Note Alex Baker's R1 10 at the 5th (b05) as the first sign that the Praia D'El Rey card was going to take prisoners
Hint at the West Cliffs back nine as a graveyard, paying off with Patterson's eight-hole haemorrhage

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Patterson held the R1 lead by a single point after a late takeover at the 16th, but Jon Baker reclaimed it at the very first hole of R2 (West Cliffs) and never surrendered it — the decisive takeover. Rounds of 38, 44, 44, 40 turned a one-point gap into 18 by Sunday, with the R3 birdie-par-birdie surge (b04) doing the real damage. Final 166 pts, the 9th-best Trophy total in TEG history, his first Trophy.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: A scrap early — Gregg Williams led after R1, David Mullin after R2, and the lead flipped three times in the front nine of R3 — before Jon Baker grabbed it back at the 7th of Royal Óbidos (b17 is Mullin's brief retort at the 6th) and pulled clear. From there it was a procession: +14, +14, +19 to finish +67, a 13-shot margin and the 5th-best Gross total in TEG annals.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: The bottom slot changed hands six times before settling. Alex Baker carried it out of R1, Patterson held it through R2's back-nine carnage, but Gregg Williams' R3 collapse at Royal Óbidos — a 26-point round capped by dropping to last at the 14th (b39) and then leaking 14 shots over the closing seven holes (b50) — pinned the Spoon on him for good. A final 29 at Praia D'El Rey confirmed it: 123 pts, last.

headline_candidates: Patterson edges out at Praia, Alex bleeds at the 5th
One-point margins and one ten on the card
Praia D'El Rey draws first blood
rounds.chosen_headline: Patterson edges out at Praia, Alex bleeds at the 5th
rounds.angle: Tight at the top — Patterson 39, Baker 38 — but the day's calling card is Alex Baker's 10 at the par-4 5th and Gregg Williams leading the Gross. Plant the foreshadow: none of these front-runners will be there at the end.

headline_candidates: West Cliffs eats John Patterson alive
Baker takes the lead and the West Cliffs wind takes Patterson
Eight holes, twenty-seven shots: a Silver Coast nightmare
rounds.chosen_headline: West Cliffs eats John Patterson alive
rounds.angle: Jon Baker grabs the Trophy lead at the 1st (the decisive takeover of the week) with 44 pts; meanwhile Patterson posts his worst-ever Gross round (+44) thanks to an 8-hole bleed of 27 shots through the dunes, including a 10 at the 13th and an 11 at the 15th. Mullin leads the Jacket overnight.

headline_candidates: Baker buries it at Seve's place
Eleven points clear at Royal Óbidos
Birdie-par-birdie: the moment the tournament ended
rounds.chosen_headline: Baker buries it at Seve's place
rounds.angle: The decisive round. Jon Baker's holes 11–16 surge (b04) blows the Trophy lead out to 11 and seizes the Green Jacket outright at the 7th. At the other end Gregg Williams drops to the bottom of the Spoon race at the 14th (b39) and stays there. Alex Baker's 10 at the 14th (b07) is the round's other crater.

headline_candidates: The Baker procession returns to Praia
Patterson signs off with two more sextuples
rounds.chosen_headline: The Baker procession returns to Praia
rounds.angle: A coronation lap: Jon Baker posts 40 to win the Trophy by 18 and the Jacket by 13, even with two 9s of his own on the card. Patterson's farewell card features a 10 at the 15th (b09); Gregg Williams' 29 confirms the Spoon.

players.arc: From a one-point R1 deficit to wire-to-wire dominance: led from R2 H1, never relinquished, takes a first Trophy (166 pts, by 18) and a third Green Jacket (+67, by 13).
The best of the rest — briefly led the Jacket after R2, runner-up in both competitions, the only player who looked capable of keeping Baker honest.
Held the Trophy lead for one round, then West Cliffs happened: a personal-worst Gross 44, an 11 and a 10 on the same nine, and a quiet drift down the table with two more sextuples at Praia in R4.
Led the Gross after R1, was last on the Trophy by R3 H14, and never climbed back — the Spoon's reluctant owner.
A four-round demolition derby of double-figure holes — a 10 at Praia's 5th in R1, a 10 at Royal Óbidos' 14th in R3, and a 25-shot bleed in R4 — but spared the Spoon by Gregg's late capitulation.

venue_notes: TEG's 6th visit to the Lisbon Coast. R1 and R4 at Praia D'El Rey (the 5th and 6th TEG rounds there — Cabell B. Robinson's parkland-meets-coastal-dunes layout, well known to the field); R2 at West Cliffs, only the 2nd TEG round on Cynthia Dye's modern links and the one that ate Patterson; R3 at Royal Óbidos, Seve's final design, the 4th visit and the stage for Jon Baker's decisive surge. Use the architectural notes sparingly — one line per course — and let the dunes/water-features supply the colour where the carnage demands it.
